title: Tender French Burgundy Braise
description: A light and flavorful braised dish from the Burgundy region of France, featuring a rich broth and tender meat.
instructions: Season the beef chuck with all-purpose seasoning on both sides.
Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown the beef on all sides, about 5 minutes per side. Remove the browned beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until it's softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots and celery to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Return the browned beef to the pot and add the red wine and beef broth.
Bring the liquid to a simmer, then cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the beef in the oven for 2 hours, or until it's tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the braised beef hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
